terms.definition: The degree to which parts of a system actively reinforce one another in a stable, mutually supportive feedback structure.
A statistical measure of disorder; in thermodynamics and information theory it quantifies the number of microstates compatible with a macro-state.
Any process or institution that imports order (negative entropy) into the system, counteracting natural drift toward disorder.
The population’s attentional bandwidth and willingness to align behaviour with incoming ordering signals.
Disorder that is absorbed, processed, and dissipated by institutional mechanisms before reaching critical thresholds.
A causal chain in which outputs reinforce the direction of change, amplifying system behaviour.
Procedures that detect and correct information distorted by noise, preserving signal integrity.
A 0-to-100 composite metric aggregating family, religious, institutional, educational, media, and economic indicators into a single coherence score.
